## [00:00 - 02:46]

Life before I was able to communicate well meant that I was often misunderstood So I'd get into a lot of arguments with people and people wouldn't really understand me and because I couldn't communicate well I didn't have the confidence to speak up So when something would go wrong in a relationship when something would go wrong in a friendship I'd be conflict avoidant because I knew I wasn't good at conflict because I didn't have good communication skills So because of that I wouldn't really tell people how I feel often and I would often just be a people pleaser And I would please others a lot. You know, I don't want any trouble. I don't want any trouble So we'll just do whatever you want to do and as a result of that I just became a massive people pleaser Even when I truly wanted something in a friendship or a relationship I'll be too scared to ask because I didn't know how to ask I didn't know how to ask in a way that allows me to maintain my pride But at the same time be loving towards the other person I didn't know that it was possible to get your way, but at the same time benefit others I just thought that the best way to benefit others is to purely just benefit other people and then just put yourself last And then I started to resent people a lot after a while. I'd miss out a lot on love You know in in relationships when I was dating I didn't know how to shine So I'd go on dates and then as a result of not knowing what to say and how to capture their attention and how to build trust and rapport Yeah, you just don't get that second call back, you know, they don't call you back and you kind of go They don't like me. I'm ugly and then you just kind of go down that path Another thing I would say is I'd miss out on a lot of career opportunities You know even as an entrepreneur when I went to pitch my business people didn't believe in me a they didn't believe in me But B what was sad was my ideas were sound. I actually had really good ideas But because I couldn't communicate I couldn't bring those ideas to life And it was such a shame one more that I'll share and it's that when I wasn't able to communicate well in my life I didn't have a lot of deep relationships in my life. There's not a lot of depth I had a lot of surface level relationships where it's kind of hey, how's your day going? Yeah good? How's your day? Yeah, yeah, it's good too and then that was it I never got to those points in the relationship where I'm able to go Hey, what's one of the biggest dreams you want to achieve this year? Why do you want to achieve that dream? Why is it important to you? What do you think your purpose is in this life? And I'm not afraid to have those conversations now and I like to have those conversations now whereas before I didn't know how to Have them and I didn't know that I could have such deep and nourishing relationships with the people around me I used to just have a lot of shallow ones. So communication has given me depth It's given me a lot of meaning. It's allowed me to shine really brightly in my career as an entrepreneur It's allowed me to navigate really difficult conversations and allow me to stand up for myself and not be a doormat It's changed my life. I think that's one of the reasons why I teach it I find that we tend to teach the very thing that has brought about the most significant change in our own lives
